rulings.Case Title: ALBERT SMOTHERS JR VS. CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DBA SAINT FRANCIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Records Pertaining To Pltf Served On The Sfpd; Statement Of Matters
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday Feb. 2, 2012 Line 1 - PLAINTIFF ALBERT SMOTHERS's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Records Pertaining To Pltf Served On The Sfpd; Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied in part and granted in part. Plaintiff's motion to quash is denied, but the subpoena is to modified and limited records from 2003 to the present. The Parties are to agree on an appropriate stipulated protective order to submit to the Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW FRAZER VS. ROMAN CATHOLIC BISHOP OF SAN JOSE
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 2, 2012, Line 12, DEFENDANT ROMAN CATHOLIC ARCHBISHOP OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the first, third, ninth, and tenth causes of action. As the subsequent employer of the alleged perpetrator, the Archbishop of San Francisco had no duty to prevent the harm allegedly suffered by plaintiff. The public nuisance claims fails as a matter of law. rulings.Case Title: PETER PAPPAS VS. BERT BENISCH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 2, 2012, Line 13, DEFENDANT GRUBER FAMILY PARTNERS LP DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the unjust enrichment and UCL (section 17200) claims and overruled as to all other claims. Unjust enrichment is not a cause of action. Plaintiff is not entitled to restitution under the UCL because there is nothing to restore to him. Plaintiff is not entitled to an injunction under the UCL because there is no threat of continuing misconduct. The other claims which are the subject of the demurrer raise factual issues that are not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD HYLEN VS. WILLIAM MITCHELL RYDER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Richard Hylen'S Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 2, 2012, Line 22, Plaintiff Richard Hylen's Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction. Hearing required. The hearing will be at 11:30am, not 9:30am. Counsel should be prepared to give a short (approximately five minute) summary of their clients' positions. Mr. Hylen and Mr. Ryder are encouraged to attend the hearing. The court may have one or both them sworn and ask one or both of them a few questions and may permit very brief cross-examination. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARK A CATO VS. U.S. SECURITY ASSOCIATES
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 2, 2012, Line 19, DEFENDANT U.S. SECURITY ASSOCIATES INC.'s MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. =(302/HEK)
